key_evidence: Pentagon declared Anthropic a 'supply-chain risk' specifically over its insistence on guardrails, and is testing four replacement providers (Bloomberg May 21)
Anthropic bearing the loss of a major government customer rather than dropping safety guardrails — a documented cost-of-conscience event (I1/I2)
Anthropic still withholding the Mythos cyber model from the EU pending compliance terms (creati.ai May 12) — consistency of safety stance, but raises access-equity question
NYU Stern BHR frames the feud as an AI-governance test case: 'the cost of conscience'
Composite 58.1 sits 1.9pt below the Functional/Established boundary (60.0); proposed 59.1 retains a 0.9pt buffer

events.label: Bears loss of major government customer to maintain AI-safety guardrails (cost of conscience)

methodology_note: INT 3.0->3.2 (consistency under sustained government pressure, I1/I2) and SYS 3.1->3.2 (structural-risk position on military-AI governance, S4). Conservative magnitude applied because the guardrail stance is a commercial-safety posture not unambiguously 'compassion' toward all stakeholders. Delta +1.0 sub-5pt. BOUNDARY CASE: proposed 59.1 remains 0.9pt below the 60/61 Functional/Established boundary — no band crossing, but boundary watch flagged. Reconstruction of published dims = 58.1 (exact).
